label: Riftbrine Mere
description: Riftbrine Mere constitutes a broad, shallow saline lake occupying a collapsed basalt basin on Brineshade’s dark uplands. Its margins form stepped black shelves crusted with pale evaporite plates that fracture into sharp, geometric rafts under tidal stress from Saltharrow’s gravitic gradients. The water mass remains heavily mineralized and opaque, reflecting the hard white radiance in stark, high-contrast glare when the haze bands thin. Narrow salt pans and wind-scoured basalt ridges define stable approaches around the shore.
